items: put a file in an application and then in properties of that file, set its build action (advanced) to 'Embedded Resource', then it can be accessed via:
you can set things like `List<CThing>` as a model for a razor page
for razor pages that are one off in a folder without a web.config:

block.text:
@* include this becuase there is no web.config for razor pages in this file *@
@inherits System.Web.Mvc.WebViewPage

- Lookup<TKey, TElement> Class
	- use this for a dictionary without unique keys

items: creates a form, disables autocomplete, allows sending files, sends post request back to /Communication/Save on submit
use listbox for instead of ddl for when needing multiple select
make an options attribute in the VM:

block.text: EnumExtensions.SelectOptions<CLegalDashboardSubcontract.eCommitmentType>().Select(o => new SelectListItem { Text = o.Text, Value = o.Value })

items: set a ascx file as a child of of 'ViewUserControl' to make it a partial that works on both MVC pages and aspx files

block.text:
[Required(ErrorMessage="")] - to make attribute in VM required
ModelState.AddModelError("", "") - to add errors
ModelState.IsValid - to check if errors
Html.ValidationSummary() - to show errors

items: return the original view with the model if errors
Use MvcConfig.RegisterArea to register the whole area instead of the area to a specific controller
MvcConfig.RegisterAreaToController to register an area to specific controller
